enrichment.summary: Kyle recaps his February 21, 2015 day living in Boulder: he's increasing cycling on the roll trainer and tracking food intake, but his car broke down twice this winter due to a gas pedal detaching from the floorboard, and a stranger at a gas station helped him improvise a fix. He also discusses writing goals, his website, upcoming cycling fitness tests, and plans to improve his vlog lighting setup.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: trusts his body and stays in bed as long as he feels tired
dislikes it because of the people at the yoga studio
optimistic he can make this summer a big writing season if he keeps up the daily momentum
frustrated the Kansas mechanic didn't fix it properly the first time

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Yesterday I rode 13 miles on the Roll Trainer.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I slept in today until noon because I was extremely tired.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: My appetite is increasing, so I have to eat more.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I’m simplifying my meals but adding more veggies to eat more.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I’m recording how much I eat and how much I ride every day.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I didn’t get much work done in the morning because I was setting up my new bike racing gear.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I drove to work today because it was snowy, and my car broke down.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: This is the second time my car has broken down in winter.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The first time my car broke down, the front wheel fell off near my mom’s house.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The mechanic said he’d seen the front wheel issue happen before with Hondas.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Today my car broke down again because the gas pedal detached from the floorboard.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I managed to creep to the gas station at 5 miles per hour by keeping the car in gear.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The same issue happened two and a half years ago on my way to Texas.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The mechanic who fixed it last time didn’t fix it properly, so it happened again.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: A stranger helped me fix the gas pedal temporarily with a metal bar and vise grips.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I don’t like going to work on Sundays because there are too many people at the yoga studio.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I’m working on my website and have paperwork to do, but it’s tedious.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I’m excited about writing and think I can do quite well with it.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I might turn this summer into a big writing summer if I keep up the momentum.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I’m making good progress on my website and feel like I’m balancing things well.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I want to do a tour of Boulder and put it on my website to reference it.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I want to test my strength on climbs in Boulder to see where I stand.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I’m planning to create a chart to track my watts and speed on rides.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I’m noticing all the little things I need to fix in different areas of my life.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I forgot to turn the light on for this vlog, so it’s dark.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I’m planning to get lights for my setup to improve the video quality.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Eventually, I want to set up a green screen for my videos.
